form_semantics.justification: All inputs have associated labels, every section uses <fieldset>/<legend>, radio groups are nested fieldsets with legends, toggles use native checkbox inputs, file upload has a label, no placeholder-only fields, and aria-describedby correctly associates hint text with inputs.

element_choice.justification: Every interactive element is native: checkboxes for toggles, <input type='file'> for avatar, <textarea> for bio, <button type='button'> for Delete Account, <button type='submit'> for Save Changes. No div-based interactivity anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> ('Account Settings') in <main>; section headings are expressed via <legend> as the rubric allows, avoiding redundant heading+legend pairs. No skipped levels.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where it adds value: aria-label on <form> creates a form landmark, aria-describedby properly associates hint paragraphs, tabindex=-1 on <main> supports the skip link. No redundant roles or attributes.

landmark_structure.justification:
Skip link,
, and
are present and correct. No
included, and the header contains a

for branding rather than a heading, which is acceptable but the missing footer is a minor gap.

content_realism.justification: Realistic name ('Meredith Calloway'), a detailed and plausible bio, and meaningful hint text throughout. No lorem ipsum or generic placeholder content.

expectations_met: Radio button groups are within a <fieldset> with a <legend> describing the group
Toggle switches use checkbox inputs (not custom div patterns)
File upload uses <input type='file'> with a label
'Delete Account' is a <button type='button'> — not a link
Legends serve as visual section headings — no redundant heading+legend pairs
Radio buttons have matching name attributes within their group
The form as a whole is a <form> element
additional_observations: Nested fieldsets used cleanly for radio sub-groups within Notifications and Privacy — avoids flat grouping ambiguity
Visually-hidden legend ('Additional privacy options') on the checkbox sub-fieldset is a thoughtful accessibility addition
The visually styled toggle (CSS appearance:none on checkbox) retains full keyboard and AT accessibility because it is still a native checkbox input
